articles.title: Protecting lions and people: the biologist dedicated to tackling human-wildlife conflict
articles.description: Moreangels Mbizah has blazed a trail in Zimbabwe as the first black African woman to found a conservation organisation in the country

articles.title: Hyde Park shooting update
articles.description: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe and his cousin Tobias Matonhodze are set to be sentenced on Wednesday after pleading guilty to charges linked to a Johannesburg shooting.

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Duo up for killing bar patron, injuring two others
more.description: POLICE in Ruwa situated on the outskirts of Harare have arrested two men for allegedly fatally assaulting an imbiber at a local bar and injuring two others following a dispute over an undisclosed matter. Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) national spokesperson, Commissioner Paul Nyathi confirmed the arrest of the pair for murder of a 30-year-old reveller. [...] The post Duo up for killing bar patron, injuring two others appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Police commend public for helping in fight against drugs and substance misuse
more.description: THE Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has hailed the general public for supporting law enforcement agencies in tackling the menace of drugs and substance misuse. The plaudits come in the wake of tips from members of the public that led to the arrest of two drug peddlers and their subsequent court appearance, conviction and sentencing. In [...] The post Police commend public for helping in fight against drugs and substance misuse appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: ILO commends Zimbabwean workers for resilience in wake of hardships
more.description: THE International Labour Organisation (ILO) has commended Zimbabwean workers for demonstrating resilience in the face of obtaining economic hardships. The remarks were made during the Zimbabwe Congress of Trade Unions (ZCTU) May 1 commemorations at Dzivarasekwa Stadium in Harare this Friday. Addressing workers, the ILO country director for Zimbabwe, Philile Masuku, said the country’s workers [...] The post ILO commends Zimbabwean workers for resilience in wake of hardships appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: ‘Zimbabwe’s informal economy players are criminalised, harassed and forced into conflict with municipal police and ZRP’, representative bodies say
more.description: ZIMBABWEANS celebrated Workers’ Day yesterday under difficult conditions, amid unabating tensions in the Middle East that have triggered fuel price increases that have had a ripple effect on transport and other daily costs. On this day, civil society in Zimbabwe issued statements calling for the restoration of the worker’s dignity, recognition of the burden of [...] The post ‘Zimbabwe’s informal economy players are criminalised, harassed and forced into conflict with municipal police and ZRP’, representative bodies say appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: NSSA processed 12,400 compensation claims from workplace injuries last year: Government
more.description: THE National Social Security Authority (NSSA) processed compensation claims from a total of 12,400 workplace injuries last year alone, a development which reflects the country’s occupational and safety gaps. In 2022, Zimbabwe recorded 4,912 workplace injuries and 70 fatalities. While injuries dropped to 4,334 in 2023 with 60 deaths, the trend failed to sustain. In [...] The post NSSA processed 12,400 compensation claims from workplace injuries last year: Government appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Corrupt cartels blamed for worsening plight of Zimbabwe’s working class
more.description: THE Zimbabwe Congress of Trade Unions (ZCTU) has slammed corrupt cartels for fuelling workers’ hardships amid calls for the enactment of pro-poor policies. The cartels are derogatorily referred to as “Zvigananda” a term used in Zimbabwean political discourse to describe a small group of politically connected individuals who accumulate substantial wealth through underhand deals and [...] The post Corrupt cartels blamed for worsening plight of Zimbabwe’s working class appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.description: Source: Govt reaffirms commitment to workers’ welfare – herald Joseph Madzimure Zimpapers Politics Hub GOVERNMENT remains committed to workers’ welfare and various laws are being put in place to legally support the observance of international labour standards, a Cabinet minister has said. more.title: Air Commodore Bere declared national hero
more.description: Source: Air Commodore Bere declared national hero – herald Joseph Madzimure Zimpapers Politics Hub PRESIDENT Mnangagwa has conferred national hero status on Air Commodore (Retired) Simon Bere who died in Zambia, where he was receiving medical treatment, on April 24. more.title: Workers deserve secure employment: President
more.description: Source: Workers deserve secure employment: President – herald Wallace Ruzvidzo Herald Reporter AS Zimbabwe joins the world in commemorating Workers’ Day, President Mnangagwa has issued a strong rebuke of perpetual short-term contracts, saying that where work is continuous, employment must be secure. more.title: ED2030: Zanu PF ambushes villagers for signatures
more.description: Source: ED2030: Zanu PF ambushes villagers for signatures -Newsday Zimbabwe VILLAGERS in several parts of the country have raised alarm over a reported door-to-door campaign by Zanu PF structures demanding signatures in support of Constitutional Amendment No 3 Bill (CAB3). more.title: ‘Translate workplace codes to 16 official languages’
more.description: Source: ‘Translate workplace codes to 16 official languages’ -Newsday Zimbabwe THE High Court has delivered a landmark ruling ordering the mandatory translation of all employment codes of conduct from English to every constitutionally recognised official language of the country, declaring the current English-only system a violation of fundamental workers’ rights. Justice Regis Dembure gave the [...] The post ‘Translate workplace codes to 16 official languages’ appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Workers’ Day gloomy as wages, conditions worsen
more.description: Source: Workers’ Day gloomy as wages, conditions worsen -Newsday Zimbabwe WORKERS across Zimbabwe commemorate International Workers’ Day today under a cloud of deepening economic hardships, with unions warning that deteriorating conditions, low wages and shrinking labour rights have stripped the day of its celebratory mood. This year’s commemorations come amid rising tensions in the labour [...] The post Workers’ Day gloomy as wages, conditions worsen appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .
